bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.brief: Performs a case-insensitive equality comparison between two String objects.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.details: This operator overload compares two String instances without regard to character case by leveraging the standard C library function \u0027strcasecmp\u0027. It retrieves the underlying C-style strings from each String object using \u0027c_str()\u0027 and passes them to \u0027strcasecmp\u0027, which performs a case-insensitive lexical comparison. The function returns true if the comparison indicates equality (i.e., \u0027strcasecmp\u0027 returns zero), ensuring consistent case-insensitive behavior across different parts of the codebase. This implementation replaces the previously commented-out boost.iequals method, favoring a standard C approach for portability and simplicity.

params.rhs: The second String object to compare.
params.lhs: The first String object to compare.

bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.returns: True if both String objects are equal ignoring case; false otherwise.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.notes: The function relies on \u0027strcasecmp\u0027, which is POSIX-specific and may not be available on all platforms. In such cases, alternative case-insensitive comparison methods should be considered. This operator provides a straightforward way to compare String objects in a case-insensitive manner, useful in scenarios like user input validation, name matching, or search functionalities where case should not affect equality.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.rationale: This function is used in contexts where case-insensitive string comparison is required, such as matching user input, comparing names in social features, or searching within collections without regard to case differences. Its implementation ensures consistent and efficient comparisons, making it suitable for general-purpose equality checks in the application.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.name: operator==

bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.brief: Checks if a String object is equal to a given C-style string.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.details: This operator function compares a String object with a C-style string for equality. It first verifies whether the C-style string pointer is non-null. If it is, the function constructs a temporary String object from the C-style string and compares it to the lhs String object using the String class’s equality operator, which performs a case-insensitive comparison. If the C-style string pointer is null, the function returns false, indicating that the String object cannot be equal to a null pointer. This approach ensures safe comparison even when the C-style string is null, preventing potential dereferencing issues. The comparison leverages the String class's enhanced features, such as case-insensitive equality, providing flexible and reliable string comparisons in various contexts.

params.rhs: The C-style string on the right-hand side of the equality operator.
params.lhs: The String object on the left-hand side of the equality operator.

bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.returns: True if the String object is equal to the C-style string (ignoring case); false otherwise.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.notes: The function performs a case-insensitive comparison, which is useful in scenarios where string matching should ignore letter case, such as user input validation or name comparisons.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.rationale: This operator facilitates intuitive and safe comparison between String objects and C-style strings, especially in contexts requiring case-insensitive matching, making it suitable for user-facing features and collection searches.
bool operator==(const String &lhs, const.name: operator==

bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.brief: Checks whether two String objects are not equal.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.details: This inequality operator function determines if two String instances differ by internally invoking the equality operator and negating its result. It relies on the String class's comparison logic, which may include case-insensitive comparisons or other custom behaviors. By wrapping the equality check, it provides a simple and intuitive way to assess inequality, ensuring consistency with the class's comparison semantics. The function does not perform any additional processing beyond this logical negation, making it efficient and straightforward.

params.rhs: The second String object to compare.
params.lhs: The first String object to compare.

bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.returns: True if the two String objects are not equal; otherwise, false.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.notes: This operator relies on the String class's equality operator, which may include case-insensitive or other specialized comparison behaviors. It provides a natural syntax for inequality checks, consistent with standard C++ conventions.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.rationale: This function is used when a clear and concise way to determine inequality between String objects is needed, such as in conditional statements or algorithms that depend on string comparisons. It simplifies code readability and maintains consistency with other comparison operators.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.name: operator!=

bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.brief: Checks whether a String object is not equal to a C-style string.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.details: This inequality operator function determines if a String object differs from a given C-style string by internally calling the equality operator (==) and negating its result. It leverages the existing comparison logic of the String class, which may include case-insensitive comparisons or other extended features, to perform an accurate inequality check. This approach ensures code simplicity and consistency when comparing String objects with C-style strings, facilitating clear and concise conditional expressions.

params.rhs: The C-style string on the right-hand side of the comparison.
params.lhs: The String object on the left-hand side of the comparison.

bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.returns: True if the String object is not equal to the C-style string; false otherwise.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.notes: This operator relies on the existing equality operator for the String class, ensuring that any extended comparison features are consistently applied. It provides a straightforward way to perform inequality checks without explicitly negating the equality operator in client code.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.rationale: This function simplifies inequality comparisons between String objects and C-style strings, making conditional logic more readable and less error-prone in code that frequently performs such comparisons, especially in algorithms and validation routines.
bool operator!=(const String &lhs, const.name: operator!=
